entities.name: GROUNDNUT
entities.description: Major food and oil crop strategic for food security and nutrition in Burkina Faso

entities.name: BURKINA FASO
entities.description: Country where groundnut is a strategic crop for food security and income

entities.name: FOOD SECURITY
entities.description: The state of having reliable access to a sufficient quantity of affordable, nutritious food

entities.name: NUTRITION
entities.description: The process of providing or obtaining the food necessary for health growth

entities.name: CASH INCOME
entities.description: Money earned from the sale of crops

entities.name: GENE EFFECT
entities.description: The influence of genes on the phenotype of an organism

entities.name: GENETIC VARIATION
entities.description: The difference in DNA among individuals or populations

entities.name: YIELD
entities.description: The amount of agricultural product produced per unit area

entities.name: YIELD COMPONENT TRAITS
entities.description: Specific traits that contribute to the overall yield of a crop

entities.name: BREEDING METHODS
entities.description: Techniques used to improve the genetic makeup of crops

entities.name: DIALLEL MATING
entities.description: A mating design used to evaluate combining ability and genetic variance

entities.name: LATTICE SQUARE DESIGN
entities.description: An experimental design used to reduce field heterogeneity

entities.name: ICRISAT-MALI EXPERIMENTAL FIELD
entities.description: The site where groundnut parents and progenies were evaluated

entities.name: GENERAL COMBINING ABILITY
entities.description: The average performance of a parent in a population of hybrids

entities.name: SPECIFIC COMBINING ABILITY
entities.description: The performance of a specific hybrid relative to the expected performance based on GCA

entities.name: INHERITANCE
entities.description: The process by which genetic information is passed from parent to offspring

entities.name: MATERNAL EFFECTS
entities.description: The influence of the maternal parent's genotype or environment on the offspring's phenotype

entities.name: RECIPROCAL EFFECTS
entities.description: Differences in offspring resulting from switching the male and female parents

entities.name: OIL CONTENT
entities.description: The amount of oil present in the groundnut seeds

entities.name: DAYS TO FLOWERING
entities.description: The time from planting to the onset of flowering

entities.name: POD SETTING RATE
entities.description: The rate at which flowers develop into pods

entities.name: HUNDRED POD WEIGHT
entities.description: The weight of one hundred pods

entities.name: POD LENGTH
entities.description: The longitudinal measurement of the pod

entities.name: POD WIDTH
entities.description: The transverse measurement of the pod

entities.name: SHELL LENGTH
entities.description: The length of the seed shell

entities.name: SHELL WIDTH
entities.description: The width of the seed shell

entities.name: OLEIC ACID CONTENT
entities.description: The concentration of oleic acid in the oil

entities.name: HUNDRED SEED WEIGHT
entities.description: The weight of one hundred seeds

entities.name: POD YIELD PER HECTARE
entities.description: The total weight of pods produced per hectare

entities.name: SEED YIELD PER HECTARE
entities.description: The total weight of seeds produced per hectare

entities.name: LINOLEIC ACID CONTENT
entities.description: The concentration of linoleic acid in the oil

entities.name: ADDITIVE GENE EFFECTS
entities.description: The cumulative effect of individual alleles on a trait

entities.name: NON-ADDITIVE GENE EFFECTS
entities.description: Effects resulting from dominance or epistasis

entities.name: NUCLEAR INTERACTION EFFECTS
entities.description: Interactions between alleles in the nuclear genome

entities.name: CYTOPLASMIC INTERACTION EFFECTS
entities.description: Interactions involving the cytoplasm and nuclear genome

entities.name: MITOCHONDRIAL DNA
entities.description: Genetic material located in the mitochondria

entities.name: BROAD SENSE HERITABILITY
entities.description: The proportion of phenotypic variance due to all genetic factors
